questions.text: The diverse range of abilities needed to produce a new-media project is called the:
questions.explanation: The term **multimedia skillset** refers to the various specialized abilities required to successfully execute a new-media project.

questions.text: The person responsible for the overall development and implementation of a project as well as for the day-to-day operations is the:
questions.explanation: A **project manager** (or program manager) oversees both the high-level implementation and the daily tasks required to keep a project on track.

questions.text: The most appropriate title for the person whose job it is to look at the overall content of a project, create a structure, determine the design elements, and assign media to the content is the:
questions.explanation: The **multimedia designer** is responsible for the overall visual and structural design, ensuring media types match the project's goals.

questions.text: The work of a(n) ________ is best when it is "transparent" — never noticed by the user.
questions.explanation: Good **interface design** should feel intuitive and invisible, allowing the user to focus on content without being distracted by the navigation itself.

questions.text: The most appropriate title for the person whose job it is to devise the navigation pathways and content maps is the:
questions.explanation: Navigation pathways and content maps are core components of **interface design**, determining how a user moves through the project.

questions.text: The most appropriate title for the person whose job it is to structure content, determine user pathways and feedback, and select presentation media based on an awareness of the strengths of the many separate media that make up multimedia is the:
questions.explanation: An **information designer** specializes in organizing and structuring information effectively across different media formats.

questions.text: The job of a ________ is to create character, action, point of view, and interactivity, write proposals, script voice-overs and actors' narrations, and text screens.
questions.explanation: A **multimedia writer** handles diverse writing tasks, including scripting for voice-overs, creating character narratives, and interactive elements.

questions.text: The most appropriate title for the person whose job it is to integrate all the multimedia elements of a project into a seamless whole using an authoring system or programming language is the:
questions.explanation: The **multimedia programmer** uses code or authoring software to stitch together assets like video, sound, and text into a functional application.

questions.text: Activities that help a group and its members function at optimum levels of performance by creating a work culture incorporating the styles of its members are called:
questions.explanation: **Team building** activities focus on improving group dynamics and optimizing collective performance.

questions.text: According to a recent study of occupations, which of these professions is NOT among the most respected in the United States?
questions.explanation: In several occupational prestige studies, lawyers often rank lower in public respect compared to scientists, doctors, and dentists.

questions.text: At Microsoft Corporation, the product manager:

choices.label: B
choices.text: represents the product to the outside world.

choices.label: D
choices.text: acquires the assets used in the project.

choices.label: E
choices.text: ensures that the project does not go over budget.

questions.explanation: At companies like Microsoft, product managers often act as the public face and advocate for the product externally.

questions.text: Which of these is NOT likely to be the responsibility of a project manager?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Manage the overall development and implementation of a project.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Oversee budgets, schedules, creative sessions, and team dynamics.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Act as the "glue" that holds the project together.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Understand the strengths and limitations of hardware and software.

choices.label: E
choices.text: Develop extensions to the authoring system.

questions.explanation: Developing extensions to an authoring system is a technical programming task, not a management responsibility.

questions.text: Which of these is NOT likely to be the responsibility of a multimedia designer?

choices.label: B
choices.text: Create budgets and timelines for the project.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Ensure the visual consistency of the project.

questions.explanation: Budgeting and scheduling are typical responsibilities of a project manager, not a designer.

questions.text: According to one quote, the multimedia designer is sometimes called a(n):
questions.explanation: The term **information designer** emphasizes the structural and organizational aspects of the multimedia design role.

questions.text: According to one quote, from an interactive standpoint, many multimedia projects are too:
questions.explanation: Critics often note that many projects lack deep interactivity and remain primarily **passive** for the user.

questions.text: An interface should:

choices.label: B
choices.text: provide control to the people who use it.

choices.label: C
choices.text: allow the user to move about within the project.

choices.label: D
choices.text: provide access to the "media" in the project.

questions.explanation: A high-quality interface must fulfill all these requirements to ensure a seamless and empowering user experience.

questions.text: According to one quote, from a visual perspective, interface design most closely parallels:
questions.explanation: Interface design is compared to **mapmaking** because it involves creating a visual guide for navigation and orientation.

questions.text: According to one quote, interface designers should:

choices.label: D
choices.text: know how to do user testing.

questions.explanation: Interface design is a multidisciplinary field requiring technical, creative, and user-centered skills.

questions.text: Multimedia writers are typically involved in writing all of the following EXCEPT:
questions.explanation: Authoring-language scripts are generally written by programmers, whereas multimedia writers focus on prose and dialogue.

questions.text: Writing for multimedia can be more difficult than writing for other media because:

choices.label: A
choices.text: character development is much more critical.

choices.label: B
choices.text: the dramatic structures of multimedia are much more confined.

choices.label: C
choices.text: multimedia development cycles are much shorter.

choices.label: D
choices.text: writers must think in smaller, more discrete and interconnected units.

questions.explanation: Non-linear, interactive writing requires breaking content into discrete pieces that can be accessed in various sequences.

questions.text: Which of these is NOT a necessary capability for a multimedia video specialist?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Skills in managing all phases of video production.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Familiarity with the tools and techniques used for digital video editing on computers.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Ability to incorporate all the sophisticated video effects into a multimedia production.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Ability to make a video look larger than it really is.

choices.label: E
choices.text: Familiarity with interactivity and how it will affect the video.

questions.explanation: Making a video "look larger than it is" is not a standard technical requirement compared to production and interactivity skills.

questions.text: Which of the following is probably NOT a consideration of a multimedia audio specialist?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The sampling rate at which the audio will be delivered.

choices.label: B
choices.text: How much space is available for *all* audio?

choices.label: C
choices.text: Which authoring system or programming language to use.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Locating and selecting suitable music and talent.

choices.label: E
choices.text: Digitizing and editing recorded material into computer files.

questions.explanation: Choosing the authoring system is typically the role of the lead programmer or project manager, not the audio specialist.

questions.text: According to one quote, the most important skill a multimedia programmer can bring to a team is the ability to:

choices.label: B
choices.text: control peripheral devices such as laserdisc players.

choices.label: C
choices.text: manage complex timing, transitions, and record keeping.

choices.label: D
choices.text: coax extra performance from multimedia authoring and programming systems.

choices.label: E
choices.text: act as a teacher and technical coach to the team.

questions.explanation: High-level programming in a team environment involves mentoring and enabling others technically.

questions.text: Producing multimedia for the Web is different from producing for CD-ROM because:

choices.label: A
choices.text: the Web development industry is much more focused on sales and marketing.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Web development is much better adapted to larger, longer media.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Web design is much closer to print design; multimedia is more like film.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Web interface design is much more complex than CD-ROM interface design.

choices.label: E
choices.text: a Web site is never finished, but is always available for changes.

questions.explanation: Unlike physical media like CD-ROMs which are final once pressed, Web sites are living documents that can be updated continuously.

questions.text: The diverse range of abilities needed to produce a new-media project is called the:
questions.explanation: The multimedia skillset refers to the diverse range of abilities, including design, programming, and writing, required for new-media production.

questions.text: The person responsible for the overall development and implementation of a project as well as for the day-to-day operations is the:
questions.explanation: A project manager is responsible for the overall development, implementation, and daily operations of a multimedia project.

questions.text: The most appropriate title for the person whose job it is to look at the overall content of a project, create a structure, determine the design elements, and assign media to the content is the:
questions.explanation: A multimedia designer handles the visual and structural elements, determining how content and media are organized.

questions.text: The most appropriate title for the person whose job it is to devise the navigation pathways and content maps is the:
questions.explanation: Interface designers focus on how users navigate through the software or application.

questions.text: The most appropriate title for the person whose job it is to structure content, determine user pathways and feedback, and select presentation media based on an awareness of the strengths of the many separate media that make up multimedia is the:
questions.explanation: An information designer structures content specifically by selecting the most effective media for each piece of information.

questions.text: The job of a ________ is to create character, action, point of view, and interactivity, write proposals, script voice-overs and actors' narrations, and text screens.
questions.explanation: Multimedia writers handle the scripting, narration, and textual components of the project.

questions.text: The most appropriate title for the person whose job it is to integrate all the multimedia elements of a project into a seamless whole using an authoring system or programming language is the:
questions.explanation: The multimedia programmer (or interactive programmer) uses code or authoring software to unite all media assets.

questions.text: Activities that help a group and its members function at optimum levels of performance by creating a work culture incorporating the styles of its members are called:
questions.explanation: Team building involves creating a cohesive and productive work environment that utilizes individual team members' strengths.
